provincial_laboratory_en: Thirteen first samples were collected. Two were analysed by RDT with negative results for Dengue, MPOX, Malaria, Yersinia pestis, COVID-19, Rotavirus, and Cholera.

As of 18 May 2026, 27 samples from new suspected cases were received and all were analysed, with no re-sampling analysed. There were 18 confirmed cases that day and 3 cases under verification. Forty samples were pending analysis at the Ituri laboratory, and 54 samples from Mongbwalu awaited shipment. Cumulative confirmed cases totalled 33 across four laboratories.

As of 18 May 2026, 84 samples were carried over. Forty-six new suspected case samples arrived, totaling 130 to analyze. Thirty-one samples were analyzed that day, with no re-sampling. There were 16 confirmed cases (12 from one health zone, 4 without forms). Cumulative confirmed cases reached 51 across three laboratories.
A total of 109 samples were collected and analysed, with a positivity rate of 33%.

35 samples analyzed: 12 positive, 13 negative, 10 invalid. 49 carried over + 92 new = 141 to analyze. 102 samples sent to Kinshasa for sequencing (20 May). Cumulative received: 218. Re-sampling: 3 samples (2 positive, 1 not analyzed). Cumulative analyzed: 134; cumulative positives: 64.

Sixteen samples were received at Beni laboratory. Seven were transmitted to Goma (4 arrived at INRB Goma, 3 were en route). Nine samples were still held at INRB Beni awaiting transport for shipment to the analysis laboratory.
A total of 18 samples (17 blood, 1 swab) were tested across seven health zones. Four samples tested positive, all from Mongbwalu.
Eleven samples were collected and analysed, none of which were positive for Ebola Virus Disease (EVD). A total of 159 results were still pending due to a lack of reagents.
